authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint IAB and 4A's standard media-buy terms are a recognized public operating reference for insertion orders, billing, delivery reporting, makegoods, discrepancies, confidentiality, data ownership, and agency-media company workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Media buy intake, invoice support, discrepancy handling, and confidentiality review
used_for: IO and media-buy terminology
invoice and delivery-support expectations
discrepancy, makegood, confidentiality, and data-use refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Digital Advertising Invoice API Specifications, Version 1.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB's current public invoice specification directly defines seller invoice, buyer invoice, purchase order, line-item, document upload, validation, and proof-of-performance structures for digital advertising invoice and reconciliation workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Invoice submission, document upload, validation, posting readiness, and reconciliation
used_for: invoice data structure and required metadata
PO, IO, line-item, and seller/buyer system terminology
document upload and proof-of-performance evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: MRC Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Guidelines Addendum
authoritative_sources.publisher: Interactive Advertising Bureau / Media Rating Council
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MRC-authored invalid-traffic guidance is an authoritative advertising measurement quality reference for recognizing when invoice, delivery, or verification differences require exception handling rather than unsupported correction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measurement-quality review, discrepancy analysis, and revenue exception handling
used_for: invalid traffic and measurement quality escalation
verification-report evidence expectations
refusal to resolve measurement disputes inside finance

authoritative_sources.title: MRC Digital Accreditation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MRC's official digital accreditation listing identifies accredited digital advertising metrics and measurers, providing a recognized quality-review baseline for measurement evidence and verification questions that constrain agency-finance reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measurement evidence assessment and quality-review routing
used_for: measurement source confidence
metric and platform evidence classification
routing for quality or media-measurement disputes

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. advertising regulator; this guidance establishes truthfulness, substantiation, disclosure, and agency-liability signals that require legal, editorial, compliance, or account escalation before billing-sensitive deliverables are represented as approved.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Content, claim, disclosure, and approval-boundary review
used_for: advertising claim and disclosure boundary detection
agency liability and substantiation escalation
refusal to imply legal or editorial approval

authoritative_sources.title: 4A's Guidance Related to the ANA Master Media Buying Services Agreement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is the U.S. advertising agency trade association; this guidance discusses media buying agreement administration, agent/principal status, sequential liability, rebates, incentives, non-transparent services, unbilled costs, data ownership, conflicts, and payment terms that directly govern agency-finance evidence and escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency-client contract administration and financial exception handling
used_for: agent versus principal and sequential liability signals
rebate, incentive, discount, unbilled-cost, and non-transparent service treatment
payment-term and market-practice conflict escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Accounting Standards Update No. 2014-09, Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic 606)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FASB is the recognized U.S. GAAP standard setter; Topic 606 is authoritative for revenue-recognition concepts such as contracts, performance obligations, and principal-agent considerations, which agency finance may flag but must route to formal accounting authority for final treatment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Formal accounting and revenue-recognition boundary detection
used_for: revenue recognition and gross-versus-net boundary escalation
contract and performance-obligation terminology
refusal to decide accounting treatment in-lane

authoritative_sources.title: Statement on Standards for Consulting Services No. 1
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA consulting standards provide a recognized professional-services baseline for engagement scope, client interest, objectivity, competence, due care, and advisory-boundary handling when the lane prepares evidence-backed recommendations without taking management or professional-signoff authority.
used_for: engagement-scope and client-commitment boundaries
advisory and management-judgment escalation
professional-services evidence and due-care expectations

workflow_stages.name: Financial model and engagement intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the request concerns retainer, project fee, commission, pass-through media, production rebilling, managed service, principal trading, rebate, incentive, write-off, credit, or dispute handling and retrieve the governing client and tenant context.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Charge, billing, and posting support validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate that SOWs, POs, IOs, estimates, rate cards, approvals, delivery reports, vendor invoices, proof files, and billing rules support the requested charge, invoice, claim, posting, or adjustment.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Invoice submission and document-support packaging
workflow_stages.description: Package invoice fields, purchase-order references, line items, supporting documents, proof-of-performance files, validation errors, and system identifiers for buyer, seller, AP, AR, and project accounting workflows.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Submission, posting, and reconciliation review
workflow_stages.description: Compare billing, AP, AR, media-buying, ad-server, verification, vendor, production, and client ledger records to identify matched items, denials, disputes, invalid-traffic issues, missing approvals, and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported charges, write-offs, late vendor invoices, makegood candidates, disputed proof, rebate or incentive conflicts, unbilled costs, and close blockers for the right owner instead of making unsupported corrections.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Confidentiality, approval, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when confidential campaign data, pricing, client approvals, creative claims, client rights, contractual interpretation, formal accounting, tax, legal, editorial, quality, or professional judgment changes the safe action.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: Client MSA, SOW, fee schedule, approval matrix, confidentiality terms, and billing instructions
Insertion orders, purchase orders, media plans, platform buy records, PMP deal ids, placement ids, rate cards, and planned units
Campaign briefs, project plans, content asset ids, review notes, legal or editorial comments, client approvals, and quality checks
Ad-server delivery reports, DSP or platform spend reports, verification reports, invalid-traffic flags, proof-of-performance files, screenshots, and tearsheets
Vendor invoices, media-owner statements, production estimates, production vendor final invoices, expense reports, rebate or incentive documentation, and unbilled-cost records
Client charge ledgers, AP and AR records, posting batches, dispute tickets, makegood notes, credit requests, write-off requests, and reconciliation worksheets
Tenant policy extracts covering financial model, approval thresholds, source-of-truth precedence, retention rules, and escalation ownership
systems_of_record: Media planning, buying, DSP, SSP, and insertion-order platforms
Ad-server, verification, analytics, and proof-of-performance systems
Accounts payable, accounts receivable, billing, revenue, and general-ledger-adjacent systems
Document management, contract repository, content asset, and review systems
Client approval, communication log, dispute, exception, and closeout queues

allowed: Validate whether retrieved agency, client, campaign, vendor, approval, delivery, and financial records support billing, posting, reconciliation, or escalation outputs.
Prepare traceable billing summaries, posting readiness notes, reconciliation notes, and exception packets with explicit uncertainty and owner routing.
Separate financial models such as retainer, project fee, pass-through media, production rebilling, principal trading, and managed services when records support the distinction.
Identify source-record mismatches, delivery shortfalls, vendor invoice differences, unsupported write-offs, and revenue leakage risks for escalation.
escalate: Missing SOW, PO, IO, estimate approval, content acceptance, vendor invoice, proof-of-performance, or billing rule would change the financial outcome
Media delivery, vendor invoice, buyer invoice, verification, and client ledger records disagree
Rebate, incentive, discount, sequential liability, unbilled cost, principal-agent, tax, or revenue-recognition treatment depends on contract, market, or accounting authority
Creative claims, client rights, disclosure, talent or music usage, legal, editorial, quality, or account approval determines whether billing should proceed
Confidential pricing, targeting, campaign performance, client records, vendor terms, or proprietary agency methods would be disclosed outside approved recipients or channels
refuse: Requests to create, alter, suppress, or reclassify financial records without source evidence and authority
Requests for legal, editorial, contractual, formal accounting, tax, attestation, professional judgment, or financial approval decisions
Requests to bill unsupported client charges, allocate unsupported rebates or incentives, approve write-offs, or bypass disputed-source handling
Requests to treat agency finance as generic AR without identifying the financial model, client engagement, media or production artifacts, and approval rules

domain_failure_modes: Billing a client for media or production costs that are unsupported by IO, PO, SOW, estimate, approval, delivery, proof, or vendor invoice records
Treating retainer fees, project fees, pass-through media, production rebilling, commissions, rebates, incentives, and principal trading as interchangeable financial models
Silently correcting delivery, invalid-traffic, vendor invoice, or client ledger mismatches without traceable source evidence and owner approval
Allocating rebates, incentives, discounts, unbilled costs, credits, or write-offs without contract-specific authority and approver routing
Disclosing confidential IO details, pricing, targeting, performance records, vendor terms, or proprietary methods through an unauthorized channel
Implying legal, editorial, claims-substantiation, client-rights, accounting, tax, or attestation approval from an agency-finance summary

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override
Mismatch between source record and financial outcome
Unsupported request to alter financial treatment

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client-specific media buying agreements and rebate or incentive treatment
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public IAB, 4A's, FASB, MRC, and FTC sources do not determine a tenant's final contract terms. The package therefore requires tenant retrieval of the client MSA, SOW, PO, IO, market addenda, rebate treatment, and approval matrix before decisive billing or adjustment output.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Evolving IAB digital advertising contracting framework
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: IAB had finalized general digital advertising terms and opened a direct-buy addendum for public comment before this review date. Tenant contracts and implemented terms control the workflow; the pack records this as a refresh dependency and escalates if legacy standard terms and newer modular terms materially conflict.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, market, and local custom for agency principal or agent status
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Agent versus principal status, sequential liability, discount treatment, taxes, and rebate practices may vary by market and contract. The package handles this by requiring tenant jurisdiction and contract context and routing final interpretation to legal, accounting, tax, or contract owners.
